note: 20 authentic items (June 22-29) from approved domains only. Market closed June 26 (Muharram). 5 items from non-approved domains were removed in QA. No padding or invented items.

items.title: Sensex -372 pts, Nifty ends below 24,000 on June 29; auto down 2%, pharma up 1% — two-day winning streak snapped
items.summary: Sensex closed at 76,728.37 (−372.10 pts, −0.48%) and Nifty at 23,946.25 (−109.75 pts, −0.46%) on June 29, snapping a two-session rally. Auto index fell 2% (M&M −2.83%, Kotak Bank −2.71%, IndiGo sharply lower); Pharma, Metal and Healthcare each gained ~1%. India VIX rose 6% to 13.84. 1,681 shares advanced vs 2,471 declined. Brent crude declining toward $72/bbl.
items.rationale: Profit booking at 24,000 psychological level, caution over Iran peace deal durability, and subdued Q1FY27 earnings expectations pulled indices lower. Defensive (pharma, healthcare) and metal outperformance is typical of risk-off rotation.

items.title: Sensex +291 pts, Nifty reclaims 24,100 on June 22; FIIs net buy ₹5,527 crore — largest single-day FII inflow in months
items.summary: Sensex settled at 77,094.07 (+291.17 pts, +0.38%) and Nifty at 24,102.90 (+89.80 pts, +0.37%) on June 22, boosted by Brent crude below $80, easing US-Iran tensions, and IT stabilisation. FIIs net bought ₹5,526.92 crore (per DSIJ provisional data) — the largest single-day inflow in recent months. All sectoral indices except FMCG and Consumer Durables ended in the green.
items.rationale: Large FII re-entry on June 22 following weeks of net selling confirms a sentiment shift triggered by crude price collapse and US-Iran deal. IT stabilised after a 3.65% weekly crash on June 19. The session was the first meaningful foreign institutional buying episode since late May.

items.title: India-US BTA talks conclude June 24 (Goyal–Greer, New Delhi); deal not yet signed; July 24 US tariff deadline remains live
items.summary: Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal and USTR Jamieson Greer concluded two-day ministerial talks in New Delhi on June 24 to finalize the BTA first phase before Washington's 10% temporary tariff expires July 24. Both sides reviewed progress and 'explored avenues to deepen economic partnership' but no interim deal was signed. Goyal said on June 26 (London) that India will only sign if it gets a competitive tariff edge over Vietnam, Thailand, and ASEAN peers.
items.rationale: Talks concluded without a deal announcement, reflecting genuine unresolved differences on tariff framework post-US Supreme Court ruling. July 24 deadline creates urgency. A signed deal would be the single largest near-term FII re-entry catalyst for IT and pharma exports.

items.title: Jio Platforms DRHP filed with SEBI June 19; fresh issue of 27 crore shares; valuation $131–180 billion — India's largest-ever IPO initiated
items.summary: Jio Platforms filed DRHP with SEBI on June 19 at RIL's 49th AGM; Mukesh Ambani announced it as 'India's biggest IPO'. IPO: fresh issue of 27 crore shares (2.9% float), no OFS; proceeds of ₹27,500 crore to prepay RJIL debt. Jio FY26: revenues ₹1.47 lakh crore, PAT ~₹30,000 crore, EBITDA margin 51.9%. Valuation range $131–137B (JPMorgan) to $180B (Jefferies). RIL holds 66.43%; Meta 9.98%; Google 7.73%.
items.rationale: Jio IPO at ₹37,700 crore (~$4.5B) would surpass Hyundai India's ₹27,870 crore record. 2.9% float structure (enabled by SEBI's proposed reduction of minimum public float to 2.5% for mega-cap companies) keeps dilution manageable. Filing removes the 'Jio IPO is only a rumour' discount from RIL's sum-of-parts valuation.

items.title: NSE files DRHP with SEBI on June 17 for long-awaited IPO; OFS of ~6%; valuation expected above ₹5 lakh crore
items.summary: National Stock Exchange of India filed its DRHP with SEBI on June 17 for its maiden IPO, including an OFS component of nearly 6%. Target valuation above ₹5 lakh crore. Listing window: Navratri–Diwali 2026 (Oct–Nov). NSE filed DRHP days after RIL's 49th AGM Jio DRHP filing, creating the largest dual mega-cap capital market event in India's history.
items.rationale: NSE IPO listing would create a direct investable vehicle for India's primary capital markets infrastructure — previously unavailable to public market investors. Combined with Jio IPO, two mega-listings are approaching that will structurally deepen India's equity market and attract new foreign and domestic institutional capital.

items.title: Persistent Systems to acquire 100% of Nagarro SE for €81/share; secures $650 mn US tech services deal — stock -10.36% on June 29
items.summary: Persistent Systems (through Galaxy Germany Holding SE) signed SPA with Lantano Beteiligungen GmbH to acquire 21% stake in Nagarro SE at €81/share; board approved 100% acquisition of Nagarro; binding co-operation agreement signed; settlement expected Q4 CY2026 or Q1 CY2027. Deal valued at ~$1.4B EV (1.2x EV/sales, 9.6x EV/EBITDA). Simultaneously, Persistent secured a 6.5-year strategic services deal with a US tech leader worth >$650 million. CLSA maintains 'high conviction outperform', TP ₹6,520. Stock fell 10.36% on acquisition premium concern.
items.rationale: Nagarro acquisition at 140% premium to pre-deal market price and Nagarro's growth trailing Persistent's organic growth raises concerns about deal economics and integration risk. Near-term EPS dilution likely before synergies. However, CLSA's 6% EPS accretion estimate and maintained Outperform suggests institutional conviction post-correction.

items.title: Kotak Mahindra Bank CEO Ashok Vaswani declines reappointment; Board initiates MD & CEO succession process — stock down 2.7% June 29
items.summary: Kotak Mahindra Bank announced that CEO Ashok Vaswani does not wish to seek reappointment after his current term ends December 31, 2026. The Board has initiated the MD & CEO succession process. Jefferies retains Buy on KOTAKBANK (TP ₹450), noting stock has gained 11% in three years but succession creates near-term overhang. Separately, HDFC Bank's board is expected to finalise a new Chairman within 7–10 days following RBI's 3-month extension of Keki Mistry's term.
items.rationale: Unexpected CEO departure at Kotak creates leadership uncertainty in a critical growth phase. Two of India's top-4 private banks facing simultaneous chairman/CEO transitions creates sector-level governance overhang. Succession risk is a tactical negative despite strong fundamentals.

items.title: S&P upgrades Adani Ports (APSEZ) credit rating to BBB from BBB- with stable outlook on June 29
items.summary: S&P Global Ratings upgraded Adani Ports & SEZ (APSEZ) long-term issuer credit rating and senior unsecured notes to BBB from BBB-, with a stable outlook. The upgrade reflects improved financial profile and reduced leverage post Adani group governance improvements. APSEZ stock traded at ₹1,783.65 on June 29.
items.rationale: Investment-grade credit upgrade to BBB improves APSEZ's access to international bond markets at lower cost of capital. Reduces refinancing risk and enables participation in larger infrastructure debt capital raises. Signals S&P's view that Adani group-level governance concerns have materially reduced.

items.title: HDFC Bank board to finalise new Chairman within 7–10 days; RBI extended Keki Mistry's interim term by 3 months in June
items.summary: HDFC Bank's board is expected to finalise its new full-time Chairman within 7–10 days per sources (NRC to submit a panel of 3 candidates; Chairman appointment precedes CEO recommendation to RBI for approval). RBI extended interim Chairman Keki Mistry's term by 3 months in June while the search process concludes. JPMorgan retains Overweight on HDFCBANK (TP ₹990), citing multi-year low P/BV valuation and RBI's FCNR measures supporting deposit growth.
items.rationale: Resolution of the chairman appointment overhang is a key re-rating catalyst for HDFCBANK — the second largest Indian equity by market cap. JPMorgan's ₹990 target implies ~24% upside from current levels. The 7–10 day board decision timeline is imminent. New Chairman → new CEO recommendation → operational clarity = structural positive.

items.title: India monsoon June 2026: 46% rainfall deficit — driest June start in 146 years; IMD forecasts advance toward Central/NW India late June
items.summary: India recorded only 53.1 mm of rainfall from June 4–22 vs 97.6 mm historical benchmark — a 46% deficit, making it the driest June start in 146 years. IMD June 25 forecast: conditions favourable for monsoon advance into Gujarat, MP, remaining Chhattisgarh, Jharkhand, Bihar, parts of UP and Uttarakhand over next 3–4 days. NE India heavy rainfall ongoing.
items.rationale: A 46% June deficit is alarming for kharif crop sowing, food inflation, and rural consumption. FMCG, two-wheelers, and agri-NBFCs face H2 FY27 demand risk if monsoon revival is insufficient. IMD recovery signs in late June/early July need monitoring. This remains the primary structural macro risk despite positive crude-inflation dynamics.

items.title: Nifty IT fell 3.65% on June 19 after Accenture cut revenue growth forecast; IT down 1% again June 29 — sector still -27% in 6 months
items.summary: Nifty IT fell 3.65% on June 19 (Sensex -607 pts, Nifty -154 pts) after Accenture cut its annual revenue growth forecast — a bellwether signal for Indian IT demand. The IT correction on June 19 wiped out the week's prior gains. Nifty IT fell another 1% on June 29. Sector remains ~27% below its 2025 highs, with BTA signing (July) and Q1 FY27 results the next key catalysts.
items.rationale: Accenture's revenue forecast cut is a leading indicator of enterprise IT spending softness in the US — India's primary export market. Combined with Persistent Systems' Nagarro acquisition at a premium, the sector faces both demand uncertainty and M&A-driven EPS dilution. BTA deal is necessary but not sufficient to restore full sector re-rating without Q1 FY27 results confirming AI deal ramp.

items.title: Hexaware named Anthropic-authorised reseller for Claude on Amazon Bedrock — stock +6.16% on June 29
items.summary: Hexaware Technologies was named an Anthropic-authorised reseller for Amazon Bedrock, joining a select global group authorised to directly sell, integrate, and support Claude AI models for enterprise clients worldwide. Hexaware stock rose 6.16% to ₹525 on June 29, leading BSE midcap gainers.
items.rationale: Anthropic authorisation differentiates Hexaware in the increasingly competitive enterprise AI services market. Direct reseller status for Claude on Bedrock enables higher-margin AI deployment contracts vs pass-through implementation. Positive contrast to sector-wide Accenture-driven headwinds.

items.title: Lupin receives USFDA tentative approval for prostate cancer drug Enzalutamide (40–160 mg) — stock +1.97% June 29
items.summary: Lupin received USFDA tentative approval for its ANDA for Enzalutamide Tablets (40 mg, 80 mg, 120 mg, 160 mg) used to treat various types of prostate cancer. Final approval and launch timing pending resolution of patent/exclusivity status. Stock rose 1.97% to ₹2,388 on June 29.
items.rationale: Enzalutamide is a significant prostate cancer market entry. Tentative approval positions Lupin for commercialisation once exclusivity periods clear. The pharma sector outperformed on June 29 (+1.06% Nifty Pharma) amid broader market weakness, reflecting defensive accumulation.

items.title: Dr Reddy's Laboratories gains 2.3% on June 29 — Nifty's top gainer; Nomura retains Buy TP ₹1,740 post USFDA Form 483
items.summary: Dr Reddy's Laboratories gained 2.30% on June 29 — Nifty's top gainer. Nomura retained Buy with TP ₹1,740, noting USFDA issued seven Form 483 observations after Hyderabad inspection (mostly related to new facility); company expects approval and launch in Q4 FY27. Form 483 observations are procedural; final outcome and launch on track.
items.rationale: Nomura's confidence despite Form 483 (standard regulatory observation, not a Warning Letter) supports market view that DRL's facility inspection is procedural. Pharma sector defensiveness amid broader market weakness drives institutional buying in quality pharma names ahead of Q1 results.

items.title: Delhi Cabinet approves new EV policy effective July 1; electric auto-rickshaws only from January 2027; EV two-wheelers mandatory from April 2028
items.summary: Delhi Cabinet approved a new EV policy effective July 1, 2026. Only electric auto-rickshaws eligible for new registrations from January 1, 2027. Electric two-wheelers registrations mandatory from April 1, 2028. Scrapping incentive of ₹1 lakh for BS-IV four-wheelers. Delhi is India's largest two-wheeler and auto-rickshaw market.
items.rationale: Delhi's mandatory EV transition policy accelerates the shift for India's largest city market. Auto-rickshaw electrification by Jan 2027 and two-wheeler electrification by April 2028 provides a hard demand catalyst for Ather Energy, Bajaj Auto's Chetak, TVS iQube, and Hero MotoCorp EV products. Scrapping incentive adds demand pull.

items.title: Torrent Power completes ₹3,632 crore acquisition of Nabha Power from L&T Power Development on June 29
items.summary: Torrent Power completed the acquisition of 100% equity shares and convertible instruments of Nabha Power Limited from L&T Power Development for ₹3,632.35 crore. Nabha Power is now a wholly-owned subsidiary of Torrent Power. Torrent stock fell 0.98% on the day.
items.rationale: Acquisition expands Torrent Power's generation capacity through the Nabha 1,400 MW thermal plant in Punjab. Inorganic growth adds capacity but raises balance sheet leverage. The rationale is strategic — thermal capacity complements Torrent's renewables as baseload stability. Near-term stock pressure as market digests deal size.

items.title: HAL board approves ₹10/share (200%) final dividend for FY26 — record date August; stock down 1.51% June 29 on broader defence sector weakness
items.summary: Hindustan Aeronautics Limited (HAL) board approved a final dividend of ₹10 per equity share of ₹5 face value (200%) for FY26, subject to shareholder approval at AGM. HAL stock declined 1.51% to ₹4,303 on June 29 amid broader Nifty weakness and reduced BEL/defence sector momentum post-peace deal. Apollo Micro Systems gained ~6% on expectation BEL is set to receive a ₹30,000 crore QRSAM order.
items.rationale: HAL's 200% dividend reflects strong FY26 cash generation from record order execution. However, defence sector faces a 'good news priced in' phase — US-Iran peace reduces conflict premium. Long-term structural demand from domestic modernisation and export orders (TEJAS, Dhruv) remains intact. BEL QRSAM order would be a major near-term catalyst.

items.title: Waaree Energies clarifies US Customs & Border Protection ruling — no adverse inference on US operations; business continues normally
items.summary: Waaree Energies clarified that US Customs and Border Protection confirmed that Waaree did NOT export solar modules manufactured using Chinese-origin solar cells to the United States. CBP drew no adverse inference. Determination limited to a narrow subset of certain historical import entries. US manufacturing and delivery operations continue normally.
items.rationale: The CBP clarification removes the overhang from Chinese-origin solar cell concerns raised by the US Withhold Release Order framework. Waaree's 4.2 GW domestic US manufacturing capacity insulates it from import duty risk. Clean CBP determination supports Waaree's investment case for US market expansion.

items.title: IIFL Finance approves ₹10,000 crore fundraise via equity/securities; borrowing limit raised to ₹75,000 crore; CFO transition on June 27
items.summary: IIFL Finance approved raising up to ₹10,000 crore through issuance of equity shares or other securities; borrowing limit increased to ₹75,000 crore from ₹60,000 crore. CFO Kapish Jain stepped down June 26; Vikas Jain appointed CFO and KMP effective June 27. Stock declined 3.73% on June 29.
items.rationale: Large fundraise signals IIFL Finance's growth ambitions post-RBI regulatory normalisation. However, CFO transition adds short-term governance uncertainty. Equity raise at current depressed levels would be dilutive. Rate-cut cycle (August MPC) provides a favourable backdrop for NBFC cost-of-funds improvement.

items.title: Nifty midcap index -0.5%, smallcap -0.6% on June 29; broader market weakness persists for second consecutive session
items.summary: Nifty midcap 100 fell 0.4–0.5% and Nifty smallcap 100 declined 0.45–0.6% on June 29. Notable midcap losers: Persistent Systems -10.36%, Astral -7.29%, Supreme Industries -6.60%, LT Technology Services -5.45%. Midcap gainers: Ajanta Pharma +6.36%, Hexaware +6.16%, Linde India +3.77%, Emami +3.64%. Broader market weakness for second consecutive session amid VIX rise to 13.84.
items.rationale: Persistent's Nagarro acquisition and Astral/Supreme demerger valuation concerns drove outsized midcap losses. VIX at 13.84 (+6%) signals rising short-term uncertainty. Broader market breadth remains negative: 2,471 declines vs 1,681 advances on June 29. Caution ahead of Q1 FY27 earnings season (July).
